basic_details.job_title: Rajasthan RPSC RAS Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 607 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC), Ajmer has released an official advertisement for 607 posts under the Rajasthan State Services and Subordinate Services Combined Competitive Examination 2026.

important_dates.preliminary examination schedule: To be notified on the official RPSC website
important_dates.main examination schedule: To be notified after Preliminary Examination results

application_fee.details: Fee is payable online on the RPSC website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in. Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS categories will be treated as General category and must pay General category fee.

age_limit.details: General Age Limit: Minimum 21 years and maximum 40 years as on January 1, 2027. For Rajasthan Government departmental candidates: minimum age 25 years as on January 1, 2027, but must not have crossed 45 years.

qualification.details: Minimum Qualification: Must hold a Degree from any University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature in India, or other Educational Institution established by an Act of Parliament, or declared to be a deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, or possess an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government in consultation with the Commission. Note: Candidates who do not possess the required educational qualification (educational qualification, experience, and age) at the time of filling the online application must withdraw their application through the Commission's website. Submitting a false/incorrect application is a punishable offense under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) Section 217, and can lead to debarment from all RPSC examinations for the next three years. | For State Services reserved posts for non-gazetted Rajasthan State Government employees: Candidate must have completed not less than 5 years of service (whether officiating or substantive) as on January 1, 2027.

salary_details.21: Rajasthan State Agricultural Marketing Service (Marketing Officer)
salary_details.22: Rajasthan Social Justice & Empowerment Subordinate Service - District Probation & Social Welfare Officer (SA)
salary_details.25: Rajasthan Minority Affairs Subordinate Service - Programme Officer (NSA)
salary_details.26: Rajasthan Subordinate Service - State Agricultural Marketing Department (Junior Marketing Officer)
salary_details.details: Essential administrative details regarding all vacancy packages are as follows:

how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows:

Visit the RPSC official website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in and click the Apply Online link, or log in via the SSO Portal: https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in using Citizen Apps (G2C) to access the Recruitment Portal.

Complete One Time Registration (OTR) on the SSO portal if not already done. During OTR, keep a live photo of yourself, your name, father's name (if available on Aadhaar), date of birth, gender, Aadhaar/Janaadhar Card details ready.
Fill in the online application form completely and accurately with all educational qualification, experience, and category details as applicable on the last date of the notification.
Pay the prescribed examination fee online through the RPSC website as per your category.
After final submission, take a printout of the application form for your records.
Candidates who have already completed OTR before April 19, 2023 can log in through the SSO portal and apply using their OTR number/registration number.
Application window: June 4, 2026 to July 3, 2026 till 12:00 midnight. No application will be accepted after the closing date.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria & Job Requirements
content_sections.text: Minimum Qualification: Must hold a Degree from any University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature in India, or other Educational Institution established by an Act of Parliament, or declared to be a deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, or possess an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government in consultation with the Commission. Note: Candidates who do not possess the required educational qualification (educational qualification, experience, and age) at the time of filling the online application must withdraw their application through the Commission's website. Submitting a false/incorrect application is a punishable offense under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) Section 217, and can lead to debarment from all RPSC examinations for the next three years. For State Services reserved posts for non-gazetted Rajasthan State Government employees: Candidate must have completed not less than 5 years of service (whether officiating or substantive) as on January 1, 2027.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit & Category-wise Relaxation
content_sections.text: General Age Limit: Minimum 21 years and maximum 40 years as on January 1, 2027. For Rajasthan Government departmental candidates: minimum age 25 years as on January 1, 2027, but must not have crossed 45 years.

rows.candidate_category: SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS Male Candidates of Rajasthan State
SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS Women Candidates of Rajasthan State
Persons working for State Public Sector Undertakings / Corporations
Panchayat Samities and Zila Parishad employees in substantive capacity
State Government employees in substantive capacity

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee is payable online on the RPSC website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in . Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS categories will be treated as General category and must pay General category fee.

rows.category: General (Unreserved) / OBC (Creamy Layer) / MBC (Creamy Layer) Candidates
Reserved Category (SC/ST/OBC Non-Creamy Layer/MBC Non-Creamy Layer/EWS/Saharia Tribe) Candidates

content_sections.heading: RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services 2026 Selection Methodology
content_sections.text: The Combined Competitive Examination will be held in two successive stages: Preliminary Examination (Screening Test): Objective type, one paper - General Knowledge & General Science - for 200 marks in 3 hours. Marks obtained in the Preliminary Examination will not be counted for final merit. The exam will be conducted in Online/Offline mode; all questions will be objective type. Main Examination (Written): Candidates qualifying the Preliminary Examination (15 times the total vacancies) will be admitted to the Main Examination. The Main Examination is descriptive/analytical in nature and consists of four papers, each carrying 200 marks and 3 hours duration. Personality and Viva-voce Examination: 100 marks. Candidates obtaining minimum qualifying marks in the Main Examination written test will be called for the personality test and viva-voce. For Rajasthan Police Service selection, candidates holding NCC 'C' Certificate will be given preference. Minimum Qualifying Marks (Main Examination): Candidates must obtain minimum 10% marks in each paper and 15% marks in aggregate in the Main Examination to qualify for the Personality and Viva-voce Examination. SC/ST candidates are entitled to a relaxation of 5% in minimum marks.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows: Visit the RPSC official website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in and click the Apply Online link, or log in via the SSO Portal: https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in using Citizen Apps (G2C) to access the Recruitment Portal. Complete One Time Registration (OTR) on the SSO portal if not already done. During OTR, keep a live photo of yourself, your name, father's name (if available on Aadhaar), date of birth, gender, Aadhaar/Janaadhar Card details ready. Fill in the online application form completely and accurately with all educational qualification, experience, and category details as applicable on the last date of the notification. Pay the prescribed examination fee online through the RPSC website as per your category. After final submission, take a printout of the application form for your records. Candidates who have already completed OTR before April 19, 2023 can log in through the SSO portal and apply using their OTR number/registration number. Application window: June 4, 2026 to July 3, 2026 till 12:00 midnight . No application will be accepted after the closing date.

faq.question: Q1. What is RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026 is a state-level combined competitive examination conducted by the Rajasthan Public Service Commission, Ajmer for 192 State Service and 415 Subordinate Service posts. The exam is notified under Notice No. सं.वि.सं. 02/परीक्षा/RAS&RTS/RPSC/EP-I/2026–27 dated 27 May 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in RPSC RAS 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 607 vacancies have been announced, including 192 posts in Rajasthan State Services such as RAS, RPS, Accounts, Cooperative, Planning and other services, and 415 posts in various Subordinate Services like Tehsildar, Cooperation, Devasthan, Labour Welfare, Social Justice & Empowerment and others.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for RPSC RAS & RTS 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window will remain open from 4 June 2026 to 3 July 2026 up to 12:00 midnight. The Preliminary Examination date and Main Examination schedule will be notified separately on the official RPSC website after completion of the application process and declaration of prelims result.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility (educational qualification) for RPSC RAS 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university incorporated by an Act of Central/State Legislature or deemed university under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Rajasthan Government. Candidates who do not meet the required educational qualification, experience or age as on the last date must withdraw their application, as furnishing false details is punishable under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) Section 217.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Exam 2026?
faq.answer: For general candidates, the age must be between 21 and 40 years as on 1 January 2027. For eligible Rajasthan Government departmental candidates, the minimum age is 25 years and the maximum 45 years as on 1 January 2027, subject to specific conditions mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What are the minimum qualifying marks in RPSC RAS Main Examination 2026?
faq.answer: To qualify for the Personality Test, candidates must secure at least 10% marks in each paper and 15% marks in aggregate in the Main Examination. Candidates belonging to SC/ST categories are eligible for a 5% relaxation in the prescribed minimum marks as per rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Exam 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee is ₹600 for General/Unreserved, OBC (Creamy Layer) and MBC (Creamy Layer) candidates. SC/ST/OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer)/MBC (Non‑Creamy Layer)/EWS and Saharia Tribe candidates need to pay ₹400, and Persons with Disabilities also pay ₹400. Candidates from other states claiming reserved categories will be treated as General and must pay ₹600.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply online for RPSC RAS & Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the SSO portal (https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in) under the Recruitment section or via the RPSC website (https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in). They need to complete One Time Registration (OTR), fill in all details as per eligibility on the last date, upload necessary documents, pay the fee online, and submit the form between 4 June and 3 July 2026 (midnight). A printout of the final submitted application should be kept safely for future reference.
